summary: engine-net48 is a second JSON-RPC engine (same named-pipe/StreamJsonRpc/camelCase contract as net9) that renders a form by INSTANTIATING THE COMPILED ROOT FORM TYPE inside a child AppDomain (ApplicationBase = the project's bin dir, ShadowCopyFiles OFF, synthesized binding redirects) on a dedicated STA thread, caching one live instance per (assembly|rootType) and serving render/describe/edit off that single object. Source text is touched in exactly two host-domain Roslyn spots: RootTypeResolver (FQ type name via shared FormClassResolver) and SourceMetadata (SourceExplicit bold + event handlers + Modifiers/GenerateMember pseudo-props, optionally from the unsaved buffer). ALL edits are 'best-effort live mirrors': the persisted truth is always the host's net9 text splice; net48 only mutates the cached compiled instance (TypeConverter SetValue, surgical/rebuild collection reconciles, add/remove/z-order/tabs) so the PNG updates without rebuild, reporting Applied=false+reason when it can't, and stamping LiveInstanceId+LiveBuildId on every response so the host can fail-close when instance and buffer diverge. GetCapabilities today: Render=true, Edit=true, LivePreviewUnsavedEdits=false (the Program.cs class doc claiming 'edit RPCs report unsupported' is STALE M1 text). A switch to interpreting live source while instantiating compiled CONTROL types would keep the entire shell (RPC surface, DomainManager/ChildDomainConfig, StaDispatcher, Snapshot/geometry/tray/strip harvest, CompiledDescriber, toolbox/smart-tags/ImageListSerializer) and replace the core Build/CreateRoot path, the reflection FieldNames map, SourceMetadata (subsumed by the interpreter), and the whole instance-divergence apparatus (InstanceId/BuildId/DiscardLive/rebuild-to-refresh), flipping LivePreviewUnsavedEdits to true — at the cost of reintroducing interpreted-subset fidelity gaps on exactly the vendor (DevExpress) forms this engine exists for, and needing a serialized statement-IR handoff because Roslyn cannot safely live in the redirect-unified child domain.
keyFacts: RPC surface (D:\Data\Code\CSharpWinformExtension\engine-net48\Program.cs, EngineApi at ~L539-901): ALL RPCs are implemented, none report unsupported anymore. Ping; GetCapabilities (L625: Engine='net48-compiled', Render=true, Edit=true, LivePreviewUnsavedEdits=false, Notes='property edits are live; manual source edits appear after a rebuild'); RenderCompiledWithLayout; DescribeCompiledComponent (takes optional sourceText = UNSAVED buffer for SourceMetadata); ListCompiledVendorSmartTags; ListCompiledToolboxControls; SetCompiledPropertyLive; ResetCompiledPropertyLive; ApplyCompiledEdits (batch, backs drag/resize/align); SetCompiledCollectionLive; SetCompiledTreeNodesLive; SetCompiledToolStripItemsLive; SetCompiledStringArrayLive; SetCompiledImageListLive; RemoveCompiledControls; AddCompiledControl; SetCompiledZOrder; SelectCompiledTabAt; HitTestCompiledTab; AddCompiledTab; RemoveCompiledTab; SerializeImageList + DeserializeImageList (assembly-free — host routes these through bundled net48 even for pure .NET projects); DiscardCompiledLive (undo/redo/revert reconcile); ReleaseCompiledAssembly + ReleaseAllCompiledAssemblies (1.0.0 unpin, return ReleaseResult{Attempted,Released,Failed}).

STALE DOC HAZARD: Program.cs L539-540 class comment still says 'Render-first (M1): edit RPCs report unsupported via GetCapabilities' — that is historical; the implementation returns Edit=true. Do not plan from that comment.

CLI self-test modes in Program.cs Main (L20-197): --pipe, --render, --describe, --list-toolbox, --parse-meta (pure-text SourceMetadata dump, no assembly), --setprop, --setcoll, --setnodes, --settsitems, --setlines, --remove, --add, --seltab, --hittab, --addtab, --deltab, --il-serialize. Probe dirs = RPC probeDirs + target bin dir + WINFORMS_DESIGNER_PROBE_DIRS env var (PATH-separated, set by host from winformsDesigner.net48.probeDirectories; only source of vendor SDK paths, nothing hardcoded) — ComputeProbes at L879-888.

DomainManager (engine-net48\DomainManager.cs, 183 LOC): one child AppDomain per OUTPUT DIRECTORY (keyed binDir, OrdinalIgnoreCase), Entry{Domain, RenderWorker proxy, AsmMtimeTicks}. mtime change → unload + recreate (near-unreachable while pinned). _leaked quarantine list: a domain refusing AppDomain.Unload (non-cooperative thread) is remembered, retried on every ReleaseAll, counted as Failed>0 → host recycles the whole process. ReleaseBinDir removes the entry FIRST (an unload-attempted domain is never handed back — Unload aborts its threads, its STA may be dead). Invariant: released+Failed==Attempted. Worker is loaded into the child domain BY PATH via CreateInstanceFromAndUnwrap (LoadFrom context).

ShadowCopyFiles is intentionally OFF (DomainManager.Create L140-158): shadow-copying relocates assemblies out of the full-trust ApplicationBase which DROPS the .NET Framework strong-name bypass for DELAY-SIGNED vendor assemblies (common in DevExpress graphs) → FileLoadException 0x80131045. Consequence: user dlls load IN PLACE and are PINNED → user's own build fails MSB3027 while a net48 designer is open; ReleaseBinDir/ReleaseAll (host calls on last-session-close and via 'release for rebuild' command) is the ONLY cure; engine (not host) enumerates what it loaded because a session that switched source silently forgets its previous pin (codex).

ChildDomainConfig (145 LOC): child domain ConfigurationFile = user's own .exe.config (or unique sibling *.exe.config) PLUS synthesized MSBuild-style binding redirects for every strong-named dll actually in the bin dir, oldVersion UNBOUNDED (0.0.0.0-65535.65535.65535.65535) unified on the USER's version — because our worker's graph (StreamJsonRpc/Newtonsoft/System.Memory) also loads there and a version disagreement kills the control's ctor (measured: user ships System.Memory 4.0.1.1, ours wants 4.0.1.2). User-declared identities are never overridden; any failure falls back to their config or none. Temp config at %TEMP%\winforms-designer-net48\<md5(binDir)>.config.

RenderWorker (1857 LOC, MarshalByRefObject, lives IN the child domain, InitializeLifetimeService→null infinite lease): LiveDesign cache keyed 'fullAsmPath|rootTypeName' = {host Form, Root control, Type, FieldNames (object→field name via reflection over IComponent fields INCLUDING base types), ByField (field→Control), InstanceId (fresh GUID per (re)instantiation), BuildId (asm mtime:length)}. Build(): Assembly.LoadFrom → ResolveType EXACT name only (simple-name fallback deliberately removed — it once rendered a different form as yours; stale build → honest error) → CreateRoot (public+non-public ctors, all-optional params filled with the author's declared defaults, required params refused with actionable signature list) → Form roots hosted DIRECTLY off-screen at (-20000,-20000) (Form can't be a child — the net48-form-root crash fix), UserControl roots wrapped in a borderless host form → Show() + 20×10ms message pump. All work marshals through StaDispatcher: one persistent STA thread 'winforms-net48-sta', EnableVisualStyles, BlockingCollection queue, exceptions preserved across (e.g. LicenseException for exit code 3).

Snapshot() (RenderWorker L1334-1373) is the SINGLE construction site of RenderLayoutResult: root.DrawToBitmap→PNG; BuildLayoutControls (field-backed controls only — DevExpress internal sub-parts skipped; innermost-first sort for hit-testing; IsOnVisibleSurface filters non-active tab pages incl. reflective DevExpress XtraTab check, defaulting to visible on any doubt; IsTabHost/IsStripHost flags); BuildTray (non-Control field components + OFF-TREE Controls i.e. ContextMenuStrip, excludes ToolStripItems, IsStrip flag + bounds-less Items forest for tray-chip flyout); BuildToolStripItemGeometry (top-level item bounds + overflow chevron with overflow children + synthesized trailing 'Type Here' slot, TypeHereExtent=66 mirrors net9, harvests live Text for rename prefill, recursive bounds-less Children for synthetic submenu flyouts); Representable==TotalStatements (compiled render claims no gaps); stamps LiveInstanceId+LiveBuildId on EVERY response.

SOURCE TEXT — what net48 does with it today (the CRITICAL question): exactly TWO Roslyn parses, both in the HOST domain (Roslyn deliberately kept OUT of the render child domain). (1) RootTypeResolver.Resolve (40 LOC): designer file → FormClassResolver.FormClass (COMPILE-LINKED from ../engine/FormClassResolver.cs so net9-splice and net48-render can never disagree about which class is the form) → QualifiedName in reflection format; ''→fail-closed. (2) SourceMetadata.Apply (189 LOC): parses the on-disk file OR the host-passed UNSAVED buffer to recover the only two facts the live TypeDescriptor can't see — SourceExplicit per property (first member hop after owner: this.id.prop[.x]=…, with field-construction vs root-prop disambiguation across partials) and event Handler wirings (+=); plus InjectDesignTimeProperties adds read-only-ish Modifiers/GenerateMember pseudo-props via DesignerModifiers.ParseFieldModifiers (also compile-linked). NOTHING ELSE reads source. Rendering never touches source; net48 NEVER writes source.

LIVE-EDIT BEST-EFFORT MIRROR — exact mechanism: persisted truth is ALWAYS the host's net9 Roslyn splice into .Designer.cs; net48 only mirrors the same committed edit onto the cached compiled instance so the PNG updates without rebuild. Per-prop: TryApply (L1025-1105) resolves via ResolveLiveTarget (ByField fast path, else ResolveComponentByFieldName reverse-scan of FieldNames — how ToolStripItems resolve), gates the edit TARGET to Control||ToolStripItem via ResolveLiveEditTarget (the Timer gate: Timer.Enabled=true would Start() it and DoEvents would run the compiled Tick inside the preview), converts via pd.Converter.ConvertFromInvariantString; framework ReferenceConverter props resolve '(none)'/'(this)'/field names manually against the DeclaredOnly-harvested sibling set (instance is NOT SITED so Site.Name is null and the converter can't enumerate), with explicit assignability + no-self-reference + no-item-reference checks mirroring the describe offer set exactly. TryReset: pd.ResetValue if CanResetValue, else ShouldSerializeValue honesty → Applied=false 'no design-time default … until rebuild'. Collections: string Items/ListView.Columns/DataGridView.Columns full rebuild Clear+Add with DEFAULT item types (real typed columns return on rebuild); TreeNodes recursive rebuild (TreeNodeCollection only — DevExpress TreeList honestly refused); ToolStrip items SURGICALLY reconciled (two-phase pure-plan/apply keyed by field id, reuse existing objects preserving Image/events, construct new from a 10-type allowlist, prune FieldNames subtree before cascade-Dispose); string[] via pd.SetValue; ImageList via deserialize+rebuild. Every failure → Applied=false + Diagnostics reason, never an RPC error.

FAIL-CLOSED TRUST APPARATUS (1.0.0): LiveInstanceId (GUID — not a counter, which would repeat across worker replacement) changes on every (re)instantiation: explicit DiscardCompiledLive (host calls on undo/redo/revert since the instance, not the reverted text, is what renders), engine crash, control-source change, hot-exit recovery, and invisible DomainManager reload after rebuild. LiveBuildId (mtime:length) distinguishes 'reloaded the same stale build' from 'genuine rebuild' — the only event that re-syncs preview to unbuilt source edits. Host locks the form when the id changes while the buffer is dirty.

CompiledDescriber (421 LOC): TypeDescriptor-driven describe of ANY live IComponent, deliberate parity mirror of net9 DesignerDescribe — Browsable/Hidden filtering with collection + TableLayoutPanel Column/Row exceptions; '(Collection)' read-only for unhandled Content-serialized IList; Font-non-default-charset and non-standard-Cursor read-only guards; ≤64px image thumbnails; StandardValues with a DescribeContext ONLY for WinForms-assembly ImageIndex/ImageKey converters (reads the real attached ImageList — real keys on net48); flags-enum members; ReferenceValuesOf self-enumerates field-backed siblings + '(this)' root token, fail-closed when the current reference can't be named (base-class field). SourceExplicit=false / Handler=null at this layer — SourceMetadata fills them afterwards in the host domain. IsToolStripItem flag guards the host's item channel.

SWITCH SCENARIO — parts that SURVIVE if net48 moved to interpreting live source while instantiating compiled CONTROL types: Program.cs RPC/pipe/CLI shell + Dtos.cs contracts unchanged; DomainManager + ChildDomainConfig + release/leak machinery (vendor control dlls still load in place → pinning story remains); StaDispatcher; the entire Snapshot pipeline (DrawToBitmap, BuildLayoutControls, ComputeWindowOffset, IsOnVisibleSurface, BuildTray, strip geometry) — it operates on a live Control tree regardless of how the tree was built; CompiledDescriber wholesale; OnResolve probing, ResolveControlType, ListToolboxControls, VendorSmartTags, ImageListSerializer; FormClassResolver/RootTypeResolver identity (new job: resolve the BASE type to instantiate, VS-style); TryApply/TryReset TypeConverter plumbing as an incremental-apply fast path; tab reflection ops (FindTabProp/PageAt/CalcHitInfo duck-typing).

SWITCH SCENARIO — parts REPLACED/SUBSUMED: Build()/CreateRoot (today the compiled root ctor RUNS compiled InitializeComponent — the interpreter would instantiate the base/root shell and execute parsed statements constructing each field's compiled control type instead); BuildFieldNameMap reflection scan → the interpreter's own name→instance table; SourceMetadata entirely subsumed (an interpreter knows explicit assignments and wirings because it executes them); the LiveInstanceId/LiveBuildId divergence apparatus + DiscardCompiledLive + 'rebuild to refresh' UX → replaced by re-interpret-on-text-change, LivePreviewUnsavedEdits flips to true and GetCapabilities Notes change; GetOrCreate asm|type+mtime caching → text-version-keyed invalidation (rebuild only matters for TYPE changes); the 14 SetXxxLive mirror RPCs degenerate to 'full re-render from source' (or survive as perf optimizations); Snapshot's Representable==TotalStatements claim — interpreted-subset gaps return.

Plans harvested: docs/plans/vs-parity-completion-plan.md (114 LOC) — net48 rows T1.1 collection parity (since largely DONE per memory), T1.3 cross-runtime routing for multi-target projects, T2.2 load-failure UX 'especially net48', deferred XL 'third-party ControlDesigner verbs/DesignerActionList — net48-only, needs a real IDesignerHost'. docs/plans/winforms-designer-plan.md (1011 LOC, aspirational) — Phase 2 = same engine source multi-targeted to net48 with an IIsolationHost abstraction (AppDomainIsolationHost), engine matrix {net8/9,net48}×{x64,x86}, canonical design-time TFM for shared .Designer.cs, cross-runtime golden round-trip tests (net8 vs net48 same file → zero diff), note that managed type identity binds to runtime (can't instantiate .NET 8 types in a Framework process). Existing durable maps docs/maps/property-editor-pipeline.md and oncanvas-toolstrip-pipeline.md already carry net48 sections with line anchors — reuse them.

files.role: RPC surface (EngineApi, ~25 public RPCs, all implemented) + named-pipe StreamJsonRpc host + 17 headless CLI self-test modes + probe-dir computation (WINFORMS_DESIGNER_PROBE_DIRS)

Child AppDomain per bin dir: create (ShadowCopyFiles OFF, in-place load pins dlls), mtime reload, _leaked quarantine for unload-refusing domains, ReleaseBinDir/ReleaseAll with released+Failed==Attempted invariant
Synthesizes the child domain's config: user's own .exe.config + MSBuild-style unbounded binding redirects unified on the USER's assembly versions (fixes class-library System.Memory bind death)

In-domain worker (MarshalByRefObject): LiveDesign cache (Form/Root/FieldNames/ByField/InstanceId/BuildId), CreateRoot ctor logic, STA StaDispatcher, Snapshot (DrawToBitmap + layout/tray/strip-geometry harvest), ALL live mutators (TryApply/TryReset/collections/nodes/ToolStrip surgical reconcile/string[]/ImageList/add/remove/z-order/tabs), DiscardLive, toolbox enumeration

TypeDescriptor describe of the live compiled component: properties/events, standard values, reference dropdowns (self-enumerated — instance not sited), image thumbnails, collection sentinels; net9 DesignerDescribe parity mirror

HOST-domain Roslyn parse of .Designer.cs (or unsaved buffer): SourceExplicit bold + event handler wirings + Modifiers/GenerateMember pseudo-props — the ONLY source-derived facts the live describe gets
HOST-domain Roslyn: FQ root type name via shared FormClassResolver (exact reflection format; fail-closed on ambiguity)

[Serializable] DTOs crossing AppDomain + JSON-RPC boundaries (RenderLayoutResult w/ LiveInstanceId+LiveBuildId, ComponentDesc, LiveCollItem/LiveTreeNode/LiveToolStripItem, ReleaseResult, EngineCapabilities); duplicated from net9 shapes deliberately
Metadata-only read of vendor (DevExpress) smart-tag menus via CustomAttributeData — never constructs attributes or invokes actions (STA-wedge + unsited-mutation hazards documented)
VS-format ImageList ImageStream serialize/deserialize (assembly-free; the one op needing the Framework runtime — host routes it here even for net9 projects)

net48 x64 exe; COMPILE-LINKS ../engine/FormClassResolver.cs + ../engine/DesignerModifiers.cs (shared form-class identity — divergence unrepresentable); StreamJsonRpc 2.25.29, Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp 5.6.0
THE shared form-class rule both engines link — which class in a .Designer.cs is the form + its reflection-format qualified name
Existing durable map with net48 section (CompiledDescriber/TryApply/SetCollectionLive line anchors) — reuse, spot-check anchors
Existing durable map, 55 net48 mentions — ToolStrip item pipeline incl. net48 live reconcile
Delta plan: net48 rows T1.1 (collection parity), T1.3 (cross-runtime routing), T2.2 (load-failure UX), deferred DesignerActionList XL
Aspirational plan: Phase 2 multi-target engine with AppDomain isolation host, engine matrix, canonical design-time TFM, cross-runtime golden tests

portBlockers: Roslyn cannot safely load into the render child domain: ChildDomainConfig unifies bindings on the USER's assembly versions with UNBOUNDED redirects (0.0.0.0-65535.*), so our Roslyn dependency graph would be redirected to whatever the user ships — an interpret-in-child design needs the parse done in the host domain and a [Serializable] statement-IR marshaled across the boundary (precedent exists: every DTO already crosses it via the OnResolve self-assembly fix at RenderWorker.cs L88-110).

Fidelity regression on vendor forms is structural: net48 exists precisely because DevExpress InitializeComponent graphs (appearance sub-objects, layout items, ISupportInitialize chains, ComponentResourceManager/.resx BinaryFormatter-era resources) exceed any interpreted subset — switching to interpretation reintroduces the Representable<TotalStatements gaps the compiled render eliminated (Snapshot currently claims zero gaps at RenderWorker.cs L1367-1368). A hybrid (compiled render as fallback/verify oracle) is the realistic shape.

Root shell construction changes semantics: today the compiled root ctor runs its own InitializeComponent (full fidelity incl. visual inheritance and XtraForm chrome); an interpreter must instantiate the BASE type instead (VS model) — CreateRoot's non-public/all-optional-ctor logic (RenderWorker.cs L1203-1237) transfers, but an abstract or required-parameter base type becomes a hard refusal, and base-vs-derived field shadowing subtleties (already documented in DescribeOn's DeclaredOnly sibling harvest, L1395-1411) multiply.

Resource loading must be reimplemented: the compiled root resolves resources through its built .resources/satellites; an interpreter must read the sibling .resx directly on net48 (ImageListSerializer covers ImageStream blobs; the general resx object graph — icons, fonts, localized forms — is a bigger surface the aspirational plan's golden tests call out).

The design-surface-must-not-run-runtime-behavior class of problems widens: the current ResolveLiveEditTarget Timer gate (RenderWorker.cs L1446-1450) and VendorSmartTags' never-invoke/never-site rationale are the precedents — an interpreter constructing and property-setting live components one statement at a time hits order-sensitive vendor init (BeginInit/EndInit pairing, properties that act on set) that compiled init sequenced correctly for free.

Pinning does NOT go away: compiled CONTROL types still load in place in the child domain (ShadowCopyFiles must stay off for delay-signed vendor assemblies), so the whole ReleaseBinDir/ReleaseAll/_leaked/process-recycle machinery and the MSB3027 UX remain load-bearing.
risks: Stale doc comment at Program.cs L539-540 ('edit RPCs report unsupported via GetCapabilities') contradicts the implementation (Edit=true since the live-edit slices) — a months-scale plan drafted from comments alone would mis-scope net48 as render-only.

The live mirror is intentionally lossy until rebuild: collection rebuilds substitute default item types (ColumnHeader/DataGridViewTextBoxColumn), resets without CanResetValue metadata keep showing the built value, unmodelled item props survive only because the ToolStrip reconcile is surgical — any new editor added without the same care silently drops compiled-only state.

Identity rests entirely on FieldNames (reflection field map, the compiled analogue of Site.Name): anonymous/non-field-backed components (Items.Add("Ready")) have empty ids and must be excluded host-side; a future interpreter must reproduce this identity contract exactly or every describe/edit/geometry consumer breaks.

DomainManager's mtime-reload branch is near-unreachable by design (a rebuild requires release first), so cache-staleness bugs hide behind the release flow; tests that skip Release* never exercise domain recreation.

Per-response LiveInstanceId/LiveBuildId is the host's ONLY divergence signal — any new RPC that constructs RenderLayoutResult outside Snapshot() would silently break the fail-closed lock (Snapshot is currently the single construction site; keep it that way).

ChildDomainConfig's unbounded redirects unify OUR worker's dependencies down to the user's versions by design — adding any new dependency to the worker (or Roslyn, see port blockers) must be validated against downgrade breakage in real vendor bin dirs.
